sourcePaper.title: SETD1B-mediated broad H3K4me3 controls proper temporal patterns of gene expression critical for spermatid development
sourcePaper.sourcePdf: workspace/cell_papers/2025_Cell Research_SETD1B-mediated broad H3K4me3 controls proper temporal patterns of gene expression critical for spermatid development.pdf

boundedMetadata.mainClaimHint: A SETD1B-RFX2 axis is proposed to form spermatid-specific broad H3K4me3 domains that support expression dosage and timing during spermiogenesis.
boundedMetadata.copyrightBoundary: Only title-level bibliographic metadata and short paraphrased claim hints are stored; no abstract or body text is reproduced.

minimumInputs: broad and sharp H3K4me3 domain calls or inputs to derive them
Setd1b or Rfx2 perturbation measurements when available
planChecklist: Record available raw and processed data accessions before analysis.
Call broad and sharp H3K4me3 domains with a documented generic domain rule.
Measure overlap between broad domains and H3K27ac-marked enhancer or promoter regions.
Compare expression level and timing for genes linked to broad versus sharp domains.
Evaluate Setd1b and Rfx2 perturbation effects against matched stage controls.
Emit structured partial or failed status when perturbation or stage data are missing.
sensitivityChecks: Vary broad-domain width and signal thresholds.
Control for gene length, baseline expression, and annotation version.
Repeat expression timing analysis by batch or developmental stage subset.
Compare promoter-linked and enhancer-linked broad domains separately.
negativeResultChecks: If broad-domain enrichment disappears after controlling for gene length or expression, downgrade support.
If Setd1b or Rfx2 perturbation effects do not align with broad-domain genes, report mechanistic uncertainty.
If temporal expression shifts are batch- or stage-composition artifacts, mark the claim as contradicted or not reproduced.
rubricPrompts: Use the same artifact schema and verdict enum as the PRDM9/DSB fate attempt.
Separate broad-domain association from direct SETD1B-RFX2 causality.
Allow negative scientific conclusions without repair into a positive verdict.
